title: cuscuta alata
intro: Cuscuta alata, also known as Dodder, is a parasitic flowering plant that grows by attaching itself to its host plant through haustoria, eventually draining the nutrients from the host. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cuscuta alata include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and improving cardiovascular health. Cuscuta alata is often prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or infusion, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to support overall well-being.
